codeReview.summary: The feature commit fixes the earlier player-wide derivation-version flaw by making stale detection match-specific via per-match metadata, and it correctly extends the returning-player sync path to reanalyze stale recent rows even when the recent Riot window is already stored. I did not find a remaining feature-specific correctness issue in this commit beyond the separate dashboard surfacing/monotonic-metadata problems already attributed to other freshness features.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker feedback shows a recurring startup pitfall on Windows: worker-base still lets sessions attempt .factory/init.sh in ways that are not appropriate for this host, even though mission guidance says to treat it as optional/no-op without a POSIX shell.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session 9a2caa52-cea5-4c64-beac-541a7ca62514 includes python3 C:\Users\bendc\Documents\GitHub\Leagueback-WEBAPP\.factory\init.sh, and handoff.skillFeedback explicitly asks for clearer worker-base guidance for Windows.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill instructions, and commit 9c2d0f1. The implementation now detects stale rows on a per-match basis and reprocesses stale recent stored matches through the returning-player reconciliation path, so this feature passes review; the remaining freshness blockers live in adjacent dashboard/metadata behavior outside this commit.
